tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, elevated creatinine, proteinuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest impaired renal function, possible nephrotic syndrome, and metabolic derangement (possibly starvation or poorly controlled diabetes with hypoglycemia). No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent clinical correlation and further nephrological and metabolic evaluation.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
